name: gap_means_empty_external_body_unsound
code:
proof fn phi_1_gap_means_empty_external_body_unsound<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(som: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, lo: KeyIterator<K>, hi: KeyIterator<K>, k: KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        som.gap(lo, hi),
        lo.lt_spec(k),
        k.lt_spec(hi),
        som@.contains_key(*k.get()),
    ensures
        false,
{
    som.gap_means_empty(lo, hi, k);
}
reason: `gap_means_empty` is external_body and derives `false` from its preconditions — if any precondition can be fabricated (e.g., via another external_body unsoundness), this becomes an unrestricted `false` oracle

name: mind_the_gap_external_body_trusted
reason: `mind_the_gap` is external_body with no proof — its gap-transitivity ensures clause is asserted without verification, so a bug in the gap merging logic would silently introduce unsoundness

name: set_gap_spec_allows_key_removal
code:
proof fn phi_3_set_gap_spec_allows_key_removal<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(som: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, k: K, v: ID, lo: KeyIterator<K>, hi: KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        som.valid(),
        som.gap(lo, hi),
        lo.lt_spec(KeyIterator::new_spec(k)),
        KeyIterator::new_spec(k).lt_spec(hi),
    ensures
        !som@.contains_key(k),
{
    som.gap_means_empty(lo, hi, KeyIterator::new_spec(k));
}
reason: A key inside a gap cannot exist in the map — this is correct but combined with the external_body trust of `gap_means_empty`, could be used to incorrectly derive non-membership of keys that actually exist if the gap predicate is poisoned

name: find_key_none_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_4_find_key_none_external_body<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(som: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, k: K)
    requires
        som.valid(),
        som@.contains_key(k),
    ensures
        exists |i: usize| 0 <= i < som.keys@.len() && som.keys@[i as int] == k,
{
}
reason: If the map contains key `k`, `find_key`'s spec guarantees an index exists — but `find_key` is external_body, so the implementation could return `None` for an existing key, and the spec-level reasoning would be unsound

name: insert_external_body_vec_desync
reason: `StrictlyOrderedVec::insert` is external_body and ensures `to_set() == old.to_set().insert(k)` — but the set cardinality increase is derived from `!contains(k)` at the spec level without verifying the implementation actually inserts at the right position
